news.title:
CRISIL reaffirmed InterGlobe Aviation's ratings; long-term Crisil AA with 'Positive' outlook, short-term Crisil A1.
Ratings removed from 'Watch Developing'; recovery driven by improved operations post FDTL norms implementation.

news.title:
JPMorgan maintains Neutral on IndiGo with ₹4,630 target; cautions on fuel/non-fuel headwinds.
UBS cuts price target to ₹5,480; cites geopolitical risks, crude prices, and weaker rupee.

JPMorgan maintains Neutral on IndiGo with ₹4,630 target; cautions on fuel/non-fuel headwinds.
UBS cuts price target to ₹5,480; cites geopolitical risks, crude prices, and weaker rupee.

news.title: IndiGo shares dropped 3% after UBS cut the target price.

news.title:

news.title:
Geopolitical tensions lead to flight cancellations, impacting IndiGo, SpiceJet, and Air India's capacity by 20%-40%.
HSBC estimates daily revenue losses: Rs 45-50 crore for IndiGo, Rs 5-5.5 crore for SpiceJet, with rising jet fuel costs adding pressure.
news.summary:
Geopolitical tensions lead to flight cancellations, impacting IndiGo, SpiceJet, and Air India's capacity by 20%-40%.
HSBC estimates daily revenue losses: Rs 45-50 crore for IndiGo, Rs 5-5.5 crore for SpiceJet, with rising jet fuel costs adding pressure.

news.title:
Nifty dropped 312 points, rebounding 250 points after hitting 24,600 support zone but closed below 25,000 due to geopolitical concerns.
Bharat Electronics, Hindalco gained; InterGlobe Aviation, L&T dragged indices. Bank Nifty fell 1.14%, critical support at 59,300.
news.summary:
Nifty dropped 312 points, rebounding 250 points after hitting 24,600 support zone but closed below 25,000 due to geopolitical concerns.
Bharat Electronics, Hindalco gained; InterGlobe Aviation, L&T dragged indices. Bank Nifty fell 1.14%, critical support at 59,300.

news.title:
Sensex dropped 1,048 pts to 80,234; Nifty fell 313 pts to 24,866.
42 of 50 Nifty stocks declined; Indigo, L&T, Adani Ports were major losers.

news.title:
IndiGo faces 12% capacity hit due to Middle East crisis; aviation yields may weaken further.
Higher crude costs pose risks, impacting 35-40% of expenses; margins may recover post-crisis.
news.summary:

news.title:
Rising Brent crude prices and Iran-Israel tensions may cut InterGlobe Aviation’s Q4 EPS by 13% per $5 barrel increase.
Gulf airspace disruptions could impact Rs. 130 crore revenue and Rs. 56.5 crore PBT, posing material short-term risk.
news.summary:
Rising Brent crude prices and Iran-Israel tensions may cut InterGlobe Aviation’s Q4 EPS by 13% per $5 barrel increase.
Gulf airspace disruptions could impact Rs. 130 crore revenue and Rs. 56.5 crore PBT, posing material short-term risk.
